cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. Let me start by reading through the information again carefully. Leslie woke up at 5am. Then, Emily saw Leslie in class from 5am to 9am. After that, Anthony saw Leslie watching a movie from 9am to 11am. Then, Thomas saw Leslie buying cookies from 11am to 4pm. Next, Ashley saw Leslie fixing their computer from 4pm to 5pm. Finally, Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. Also, the beach was closed after 10pm. So the question is, between what times could Leslie have gone to the beach? The options are A) 5pm to 9pm, B) 9am to 11am, C) 9pm to 10pm, D) 4pm to 5pm. First, I need to map out Leslie's known activities and see when there are gaps in their schedule. The beach must be visited during a time when the beach is open (before 10pm) and when Leslie isn't doing something else. From 5am to 9am: occupied with class. From 9am to 11am: movie. So after class ends at 9am, Leslie is at the movie until 11am. From 11am to 4pm: buying cookies. So after the movie ends at 11am, Leslie is at the bakery until 4pm. From 4pm to 5pm: fixing computer. So after leaving the bakery at 4pm, Leslie is at the electronics store until 5pm. Then, between 5pm and 9pm: What's happening here? After Leslie finishes fixing the computer at 5pm, there's no mention of any activity until 9pm when Leslie is walking towards the Statue of Liberty. So between 5pm and 9pm, Leslie is unaccounted for. Then from 9pm to 10pm: walking to the Statue of Liberty. And the beach is closed after 10pm. Therefore, the only time window where Leslie is unaccounted for is between 5pm and 9pm. During that time, the beach is still open (since it closes at 10pm), so Leslie could have gone to the beach during that window. Let me check the options again: A) 5pm to 9pm - This is the gap we identified. Leslie isn't doing anything during this time and the beach is open.
